what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for a design-source image asset named vt-architects/p-1, declaring it as a JPEG blob with creation provenance and an empty link set.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob URI, mime type, name, and zero width/height values
source field set to design_source_material and an author_session identifier
created timestamp in UTC with timezone offset
Body line labels the entry as 'SOURCE design ground-truth — vt-architects/p-1.jpg'
standouts: Image dimensions are recorded as 0x0 despite the file being a JPEG
Blob is referenced via a 'blob://b2:...' URI
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured asset metadata
Uses UTC ISO-8601 timestamp with offset
Keeps a separate body line for human-readable source classification
concerns: Width and height are 0, which is likely inaccurate for an actual image
No descriptive caption, alt text, or tags
links array is empty, leaving the asset isolated in the ledger
notes: This file is a metadata stub for a vt-architects source image in the design-source channel, but it lacks dimensions and relationships.
questions: Why are the image width and height set to 0?
What visual content does vt-architects/p-1.jpg contain?
How is this ground-truth image intended to be consumed or referenced by other files?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record a design ground-truth image; the file does that as a metadata stub, but the zero dimensions suggest the record is incomplete.
intention_signals: Catalog a source image from vt-architects as a trusted design ground-truth reference within the design-source channel.
contribution: A metadata stub that preserves the blob identity, authorship session, channel classification, and ground-truth label for a design-source image.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata file for the vt-architects/p-1 JPEG image in the design-source channel, referencing a b2 blob and recording zero dimensions and empty links.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a design ground-truth image asset with provenance so it can be located and referenced within the channel memory system.
